channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkey seeks to purge pro-US Kurdish force that helped defeat Islamic State in Syria
item.description: A Turkish strongman exploits chaos in Syria to oust pro-American Syrian Kurds from border area while solidifying expansion in war-torn nation
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrians cheer end of 50 years of Assad rule at first Friday prayers since government fell
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Exuberant Syrians observed the first Friday prayers since the ouster of President Bashar Assad, gathering in the capital's historic main mosque, its largest square and around the country to celebrate the end of half a century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: ALBERT AJI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military flies missing American out of Syria
item.description: The U.S. military on Friday flew a missing American citizen out of Syria this week, after the stunning fall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad's regime. Travis Timmerman was transferred to U.S. forces in Syria on Friday, according to a defense official, and was flown to Jordan. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: The U.S. military has brought an American who was imprisoned in Syria for seven months out of the country.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hearings are done: Now the wait begins for verdict on closely-watched climate case at UN’s top court
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A lawyer for a small South Pacific island nation told the world’s highest court to heed her warning.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel eyes Iran nuke sites amid reports Trump mulls moves to block Tehran atomic program
item.description: Israel is once again said to be eyeing strikes on Iran's nuclear program as the incoming Trump administration is reportedly mulling plans on how to come down hard on Iran without prompting a war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: Saudi Arabia says that to host the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, it will build or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports and public transport in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John And Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU nations agree a crackdown on migrant smugglers. Some fear it may target people trying to help
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union countries agreed on Friday a draft law aimed at preventing and countering migrant smuggling, which critics say could be used to target people or charity groups that try to help migrants in trouble.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew is barred from UK
item.description: A suspected Chinese spy with business ties to Prince Andrew has been barred from the United Kingdom because of concerns that he posed a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ historic main mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's military is staying silent on its recent activity around Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's military kept silent Friday on its large deployment of naval and coast guard ships off Taiwan this week, with its top spokesperson quoting an ancient strategist instead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Environmental groups condemn new laws threatening soybean restrictions in Brazil's Amazon
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — Dozens of environmental nonprofits issued a manifesto Thursday condemning new laws in Brazilian states that threaten to dismantle the Amazon soy moratorium — a landmark voluntary agreement banning trade in soybeans from recently deforested areas.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria faces crisis and Gaza heads toward famine, World Food Program official says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The deputy executive director of the U.N. World Food Program has been on whirlwind visits to hotspots in the Middle East and Sudan to assess dire humanitarian situations and escalating demands for food from millions of people trapped or fleeing conflicts.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's Xi is likely to decline Trump's inauguration invitation, seeing it as too risky to attend
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese leader Xi Jinping would likely see President-elect Donald Trump’s invitation to attend his inauguration as too risky to accept, and the gesture from Trump may have little bearing on the increasingly competitive ties between the two nations as the White House changes hands,
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan says Netanyahu 'ready to do a deal' as Hamas said to concede to Israel cease-fire demands
item.description: Hamas and Israel have signaled they may be taking more serious steps to reach a cease-fire and to secure the release of the hostages held in Gaza for more than a year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO chief warns of 'huge risk' in Ukraine talks without Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte expressed concern about the idea of conducting peace negotiations in the nearly three-year-long Russia-Ukraine war without Russian President Vladimir Putin "at the table," calling the move "risky.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau declares himself ‘proud feminist’ after lamenting Harris loss to Trump as setback for women
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau on Tuesday declared himself a "proud feminist" as he lamented Vice President Harris’ loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria's liberated political prisons reveal grim reality of Bashar Assad's regime of torture
item.description: The fall of Bashar Assad's regime in Syria has provided a unobstructed view of the torture and persecution inflicted on the nation's prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American freed after 7 months detention in Syria
item.description: An American citizen detained in Syria was freed Thursday after rebels overtook the city of Damascus, toppling the rule of President Bashar Assad and releasing thousands from imprisonment according to The Associated Press. Pete Travis Timmerman, 29, from Urbana, Mo.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Florida Republican brings 'America First' bent to powerful Foreign Affairs Committee
item.description: Rep. Brian Mast’s (R-Fla.) surprising selection to be the next chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee is the latest sign of the Republican Party being reshaped by the next generation of "America First," pro-Trump leaders.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks businessman Leandro Rizzuto Jr. to be ambassador to the Organization of American States
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Wednesday that he is tapping Leandro Rizzuto Jr., former Conair executive and consul to Bermuda, to be an ambassador for the Organization of American States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The world’s largest Christmas tree farm in Oregon sells nearly 1 million trees annually
item.description: Oregon produces more Christmas trees than any other U.S. state, with 4.7 million harvested every year. The state produces 33% of the United States' Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators call on Biden to brief upper chamber on efforts to return Austin Tice from Syria
item.description: In a bipartisan push, senators have called on President Biden to brief the upper chamber to provide specific details on efforts underway to secure the release of Austin Tice from captivity in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China’s sci-fi spherical Death Star-like robot cop uses AI, facial recognition to track criminals
item.description: Chinese robotics company, Logon Technology, unveiled the RT-G autonomous spherical robot which will use AI and facial recognition to aid law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian President Lula in ICU after surgery to drain head hemorrhage, hospital says
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is recovering in an ICU after undergoing surgery to drain an intracranial hemorrhage following a fall at home in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, WGN 9
